message:
docs(readme): add discoverable Quickstart section (wa-nu4.3.9.12)

The existing README had a center-aligned Quick Install snippet at the top
and a detailed Installation section at line 236, but no cohesive guided
walkthrough for a first-touch operator. Ship-readiness DoD in wa-nu4.3.9
calls out "README + quickstart lets another engineer install and run wa."

Add a ## Quickstart H2 immediately after the Why Use ft? feature table so
it lands in the first ~120 lines. Covers install → verify (--version +
doctor --json) → first ft robot state call → watch + events/search, with
cross-links to Quick Example, Installation, Robot Mode (JSON API), and the
Supported Surface Matrix rather than duplicating them.

Distinct anchor (#quickstart) from the existing operator-focused
#quick-start section at line 340, which covers ft setup + fonts + health.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-0p5q5","title":"[MEDIUM] ars_drift::DriftMonitor::observe NaN-poisons null_rate and silently prevents drift detection","description":"# Defect

## File + line

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ars_drift.rs:162` — `DriftMonitor::observe`.

## Concrete failure path

```rust
pub fn observe(&mut self, outcome: f64, config: &EValueConfig) -> DriftVerdict {
    self.total_observations += 1;
    let outcome = outcome.clamp(0.0, 1.0);  // ← NaN-bypasses: f64::clamp(NaN, _, _) returns NaN
    ...
}
```

Same underlying gotcha as ft-761tz (disk_pressure) and ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram): `f64::clamp` returns NaN for NaN input. One NaN `outcome` then taints:

1. `self.calibration.push(NaN)` — NaN enters the calibration Vec.
2. `sum: f64 = self.calibration.iter().sum()` → NaN (NaN is absorbing under addition).
3. `self.null_rate = sum / n` → NaN.
4. `self.null_rate.clamp(0.01, 0.99)` → NaN (same gotcha).
5. `calibrated = true` with a NaN `null_rate` — permanent poison.
6. Every post-calibration observe():
   * `factor = 1.0 + lambda * (outcome - null_rate)` → NaN.
   * `factor.max(0.0)` → 0.0 (f64::max ignores NaN).
   * `e_value = e_value * decay * 0.0 = 0.0`.
   * `e_value >= threshold` is false forever.
   * `DriftVerdict::NoDrift` returned for every sample.

**Net: one NaN outcome silently disables drift detection for the remaining lifetime of the monitor.** Operators trust 'no drift' reports; the monitor is effectively a no-op.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer — the worst possible disposition for an observability/drift detector.
- Not a crash, not data loss.
- Recovery requires `full_reset()` — which nobody calls automatically since the monitor appears healthy.
- Same class as disk_pressure (ft-761tz), rate_limit (ft-7ojbj), backpressure config (ft-atcr0). The swarm has been closing NaN gotchas systematically; this is the next one.

## Fix sketch

`observe(NaN)` must be a no-op: do not bump `total_observations`, do not push into calibration, do not poison state. Return the current verdict (typically NoDrift or InsufficientData depending on phase).

```rust
pub fn observe(&mut self, outcome: f64, config: &EValueConfig) -> DriftVerdict {
    if outcome.is_nan() {
        // Surface the current state rather than poisoning the monitor.
        return if !self.calibrated {
            DriftVerdict::InsufficientData {
                observations: self.calibration.len(),
                required: config.min_calibration,
            }
        } else {
            DriftVerdict::NoDrift {
                e_value: self.e_value,
                null_rate: self.null_rate,
            }
        };
    }
    self.total_observations += 1;
    let outcome = outcome.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    ...
}
```

Also null_rate.clamp(0.01, 0.99) after mean computation is itself NaN-vulnerable — defense in depth: if for any reason null_rate is NaN at that point (future code change), snap to the midpoint 0.5 rather than letting NaN propagate. Not strictly needed with the entry guard above, but cheap.

## Tests

* `ft_0p5q5_observe_nan_does_not_enter_calibration` — NaN input leaves `total_observations` unchanged, calibration Vec unchanged, returns InsufficientData.
* `ft_0p5q5_observe_nan_after_calibration_preserves_verdict` — NaN input after calibration returns NoDrift without touching e_value or null_rate.
* `ft_0p5q5_observe_nan_then_clean_sample_detects_drift` — end-to-end: NaN then clean sequence still triggers drift when threshold crossed (no permanent poison).

## Scope

Single file (`ars_drift.rs`). No public API break — `observe(NaN)` becomes a no-op which matches the fail-closed precedent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:27.594084Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:54.105196Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:54.104932Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-0pcn5","title":"escape parser short DCS streaming recovery coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:24:14.038314Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:09.817662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:09.816968Z","close_reason":"Added standalone short-DCS streaming proptest coverage and verified it with frankenterm-escape-parser test target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-0vt3k","title":"[SEV] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology rollback check only compares pane id set, not split geometry","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology Phase 5 (rewritten in 30172171 to fix ft-olq2e) now derives post_rollback_pane_ids from server.registry().snapshot() and asserts the sorted id list matches baseline_pane_ids. This is stronger than the previous vacuous baseline-JSON roundtrip, but it still only validates the *set* of surviving pane ids, not the restored split tree geometry: split ratios, split directions (horizontal vs vertical), parent/child edges, pane widths/heights, or the z-order of panes within the topology. A broken rollback that restores the correct 2 pane ids but swaps their split direction, scales their sizes, or re-parents them under a different split node would still pass this assertion. Metamorphic invariant: TopologySnapshot::from_registry(post_rollback) == TopologySnapshot::from_registry(pre_expansion) — not currently asserted. Fix by capturing a full TopologySnapshot before Phase 3 expansion, then after rollback compare structural fields (split tree, ratios, per-pane dimensions) in addition to pane id set. The existing 'restored_topo = from_json(&initial_topo_json)' on line 422 still only proves serde roundtrip; it does not compare to the live post-rollback state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:42.691288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912477Z","close_reason":"Resolved in 4784c64f + d29498d6. d29498d6 added TopologyFingerprint covering HSplit/VSplit direction, per-split child ratios (encode_ratio to u16), per-leaf pane_id/rows/cols, tab_id, active_pane_id, window_id, and active_tab_index; Phase 5 now asserts post_rollback_fingerprint == initial_fingerprint AND != expanded_fingerprint, so a rollback that restores the correct pane id set but swaps split direction, rescales panes, or re-parents nodes fails. 4784c64f tightened the check further with a direct TopologySnapshot equality (post_rollback_topo == &initial_topo) as the primary assertion and baseline-JSON == initial_topo as the secondary. Ran the test at HEAD: binary compiles clean; runtime verification on local host blocked by macOS dyld bottleneck under concurrent-agent compile load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-0wnq3","title":"[audit] patterns: detect_with_context allocates detections before overlap and dedupe gates","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:40.555690Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:42.267406Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3611,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perf finding from 2026-04-23 hot-path audit: detect_with_context() materializes all candidate Detection objects before overlap, agent-type, and dedupe gating, so the steady-state path pays allocation and cloning cost for matches that are immediately discarded. Evidence: detect() allocates Vec<Detection> and, for each regex hit, clones rule_id/event_type, builds extracted JSON, and allocates matched_text at patterns.rs:2232-2323; detect_with_context() then calls detect(&input_text) and only afterwards drops overlap-window matches, wrong-agent rules, and already-seen detections at patterns.rs:2375-2409. Worst case: a noisy pane with large tail overlap or repeated limit banners causes many regex captures to allocate Strings/Maps only to be filtered out, scaling cost with candidate matches rather than emitted detections. Proposed measurement strategy: add a criterion bench around detect_with_context() with (1) high-overlap segmented inputs, (2) repeated duplicate banners under a warm DetectionContext, and (3) mixed-agent rule packs; record wall time plus allocation counts/bytes via heaptrack or dhat, and compare candidate detection count vs emitted count to quantify wasted work before any fix.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:49Z"}]}

Evidence: detect() allocates Vec<Detection> and, for each regex hit, clones rule_id/event_type, builds extracted JSON, and allocates matched_text at patterns.rs:2232-2323; detect_with_context() then calls detect(&input_text) and only afterwards drops overlap-window matches, wrong-agent rules, and already-seen detections at patterns.rs:2375-2409. Worst case: a noisy pane with large tail overlap or repeated limit banners causes many regex captures to allocate Strings/Maps only to be filtered out, scaling cost with candidate matches rather than emitted detections. Proposed measurement strategy: add a criterion bench around detect_with_context() with (1) high-overlap segmented inputs, (2) repeated duplicate banners under a warm DetectionContext, and (3) mixed-agent rule packs; record wall time plus allocation counts/bytes via heaptrack or dhat, and compare candidate detection count vs emitted count to quantify wasted work before any fix.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:45:49Z"},{"id":3612,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current HEAD already avoids the reported hot-path waste. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:2442-2468, agent-type / overlap / dedupe checks all  before any allocation-heavy materialization; , , and  construction happen only afterward at :2470-2487 and :2500-2511. Focused regression tests are already present in the same file:  and  around :4769-4826.  attributes the reordered gating/materialization path to commit 5dcea11e (2026-04-23). No code change needed in this checkout; switching to ft-8ku8p.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:11Z"},{"id":3613,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current HEAD already avoids the reported hot-path waste. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs lines 2442-2468, agent-type, overlap, and dedupe checks all continue before any allocation-heavy materialization. extract_captures, matched_text, and Detection construction happen only afterward at lines 2470-2487 and 2500-2511. Focused regression tests are already present in the same file: dedup_drop_path_allocations_do_not_scale_with_matched_text_ft_0wnq3 and overlap_drop_path_skips_detection_materialization_ft_0wnq3 around lines 4769-4826. git blame attributes the reordered gating/materialization path to commit 5dcea11e from 2026-04-23. No code change needed in this checkout; switching to ft-8ku8p.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:26Z"},{"id":3615,"issue_id":"ft-0wnq3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Correction: the immediately preceding comment with blank placeholders was a failed shell-quoted attempt. The accurate evidence is in the next comment: current HEAD gates before materialization, and the focused ft-0wnq3 regression tests are already present.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:53:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-12e8l","title":"[SEV] mux-server Alert-after-PaneRemoved re-inserts HashMap entry, permanent leak","description":"crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs:256 processes MuxNotification::Alert by calling handler.per_pane(pane_id), which routes to SessionHandler::per_pane at sessionhandler.rs:282. That function uses HashMap::entry(..).or_insert_with(..) — a get-or-insert. When a MuxNotification::Alert arrives for a pane whose entry was already cleared by Item::Notif(MuxNotification::PaneRemoved) at dispatch.rs:247, the or_insert_with re-creates a fresh PerPane entry. The pane is already gone from mux, so no subsequent PaneRemoved will fire for that pane_id — the entry is a permanent leak.

Concrete interleaving:
  1. Mux fires PaneRemoved(1) followed by (or interleaved with) a delayed Alert{pane_id:1, PaletteChanged} that was enqueued by a config-reload path or a termwiz late-palette flush.
  2. Dispatch loop processes PaneRemoved -> handler.remove_per_pane(1) clears the map entry (line 247).
  3. Dispatch loop processes Alert -> handler.per_pane(1) -> or_insert_with -> re-creates an entry (line 256).
  4. Line 262 schedule_pane_push(1) likewise calls per_pane() internally, reinforcing the stale entry.
  5. No further PaneRemoved for pane 1 is ever emitted (pane already killed). Entry leaks for the lifetime of the ClientSession.

Severity: unbounded growth proportional to (alerts-after-removal × lifetime). Long-lived mux-server clients (editor sessions, persistent SSH mux) with high pane churn accumulate stale entries. Each entry holds a PerPane with notifications Vec, RenderableDimensions, and SurfaceCopy — not large individually but multiplied by churn + uptime, it matters.

Fix: expose a non-inserting accessor SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present(pane_id) -> Option<Arc<Mutex<PerPane>>> and use it for the Alert arm. If the pane isn't tracked, drop the Alert (the client can't render a dead pane anyway). Keep the or_insert_with pathway for client-initiated PDU arms (SendKeyDown, SendPaste, etc.) where insertion on first reference is correct. Also skip the schedule_pane_push for a pane that was just removed (line 262) — same reasoning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:46:06.681760Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407372Z","close_reason":"Completed: added SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present non-inserting accessor and routed dispatch.rs Alert arm through it. Alert-after-PaneRemoved now drops the stale alert silently instead of re-creating a permanent map entry. Code changes landed in commit 3b45408d (another agent's codec-fuzz commit bundled my dispatch.rs + sessionhandler.rs edits into their push — my 37 lines are present in HEAD). cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --offline passed clean in 13.28s during my local verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.28","title":"[gui][test] GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite","description":"# GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite

## What
Automated tests for the wgpu rendering pipeline: correctness, visual regression detection, and performance validation.

## Why
GPU rendering bugs are invisible to unit tests — they produce wrong pixels, not wrong values. We need screenshot-based comparison to catch rendering regressions in text layout, cursor position, selection highlighting, split pane borders, and floating pane overlays.

## Tests

### Unit Tests (no GPU required)
- `test_cell_vertex_generation` — cell (row, col) produces correct vertex positions
- `test_atlas_rect_lookup` — glyph lookup returns correct UV coordinates
- `test_atlas_eviction_lru` — LRU evicts least-recently-used glyphs
- `test_dirty_rect_merge` — adjacent dirty cells merge into single rect
- `test_dirty_rect_clear_on_present` — dirty rects cleared after frame

### Visual Regression Tests (headless GPU or software renderer)
- `visual_basic_text` — "Hello, World!" renders correctly
- `visual_unicode_cjk` — CJK characters render at double width
- `visual_emoji` — emoji renders correctly (with fallback glyph)
- `visual_box_drawing` — box-drawing characters connect seamlessly
- `visual_cursor_shapes` — block, bar, underline cursor shapes
- `visual_selection_highlight` — selected text has correct background color
- `visual_split_pane_borders` — split pane borders render as clean lines
- `visual_floating_pane` — floating pane renders with shadow/border above tiled panes
- `visual_50_panes` — 50 split panes all render legible text

### Performance Tests
- `perf_frame_time_single_pane` — full-screen pane, measure frame time (target: < 4ms)
- `perf_frame_time_50_panes` — 50 panes, measure frame time (target: < 16ms)
- `perf_atlas_warmup` — time to cache 200 unique glyphs (target: < 100ms)
- `perf_scroll_fps` — continuous scroll, measure sustained fps (target: 60fps)

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_gpu_rendering.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui in headless mode (--headless)
2. Open shell, run: echo "RENDER_TEST_$(date +%s)"
3. Capture screenshot to /tmp/ft_visual_test.png
4. Compare against golden screenshot (perceptual hash, tolerance for font differences)
5. Open 10 split panes, capture screenshot
6. Verify all 10 panes contain visible text (OCR or pixel sampling)
7. Measure frame time over 100 frames, report p50/p95/p99
8. Report: pass/fail with visual diff images on failure
```

## Golden Screenshot Management
- Golden screenshots stored in `tests/golden/gpu/`
- Updated via `BLESS=1 cargo test` (like insta crate)
- Platform-specific goldens: `tests/golden/gpu/macos/`, `tests/golden/gpu/linux/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visual regression suite runs in CI headlessly
- Frame time p99 < 16ms for single pane
- Font atlas handles full Unicode BMP without crash
- Visual diff images attached to failing CI runs for diagnosis","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.252309Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:28.918315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","gui","rendering","testing"],"comments":[{"id":2635,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote taking a bounded support slice under the blocked GPU regression-suite bead: expand pure renderer helper/unit coverage in existing test modules (`termwindow/webgpu.rs` and `termwindow/render/mod.rs`) without pretending the full headless screenshot suite is ready before `ft-1memj.10` stabilizes. Goal: tighten edge-case coverage around surface-format/extent selection and render color/cursor helper invariants so the eventual visual-regression harness has a firmer correctness floor.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:45:45Z"},{"id":2637,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote progress: added focused renderer helper edge-case tests in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs. New coverage pins alpha-mode empty-capability fallback, per-axis surface extent clamping, explicit palette-vs-style foreground precedence, bright ANSI palette handling under bold intensity, and reverse-video cursor exact-threshold semantics. Validation: `cargo fmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed. `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-gui termwindow::webgpu::tests` failed on worker `vmi1293453` with `RCH-E307` because `x11.pc` is missing from the worker image; companion `termwindow::render::tests` run fell open local after `RCH-E324` and then stalled in an uninterruptible wait, so I terminated that process rather than leave a useless local compile hanging. Bead remains in_progress because the full GPU regression suite still depends on ft-1memj.10 and proper worker images.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:52:53Z"},{"id":3521,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook taking a bounded ft-1memj.28 slice while Agent Mail is timing out under DB contention. Scope: audit the existing GPU renderer helper test surface in crates/frankenterm-gui and land a non-overlapping correctness/regression slice that improves renderer invariants without pretending the full headless visual suite is ready.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:27:47Z"},{"id":3526,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large and independent-axis clamping. Remote targeted verification reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing x11-xcb.pc for crate x11. Local rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check are clean.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:37:10Z"},{"id":3527,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live-resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large/independent-axis clamping. Verification: remote targeted lane  reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing  for crate  (-I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libx11/1.8.13/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/xorgproto/2025.1/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxcb/1.17.0/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxau/1.0.12/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxdmcp/1.1.5/include -L/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libx11/1.8.13/lib -lX11-xcb -lX11 -L/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxcb/1.17.0/lib -lxcb failed). Local non-cargo hygiene is clean: rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check pass.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:38:28Z"},{"id":3604,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-23 audit: partially done, not closeable yet. Missing named test families from the bead: , atlas lookup/eviction tests, dirty-rect merge/clear tests, and the visual screenshot suite starting with  (plus populated goldens under ). Leaving bead in_progress.","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:02Z"},{"id":3605,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-23 audit follow-up: partially done, still not closeable. Golden screenshot evidence: tests/golden/gpu is missing, and crates/frankenterm-gui/tests/golden/gpu is also missing. Existing helper coverage does exist in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs (surface_format_*, alpha_mode_*, initial_surface_extent_*, resize_surface_extent_*) and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs (resolve_fg_color_attr_* and should_use_reverse_video_cursor_*). Missing acceptance-criteria work remains: cell vertex generation tests, atlas rect/eviction tests, dirty-rect merge/clear tests, and the visual screenshot regression suite plus populated golden assets.","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1p36z","title":"T1 retention_hours omits hot and warm recorder lifecycle windows","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:51:36.863931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:09.964973Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:09.964645Z","close_reason":"Landed in f6c3057f: T1 retention_hours now includes hot + warm + archived retention windows, with unit + proptest contract updates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -64,7 +64,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-89fl5","title":"[bug] BackpressureManager::evaluate tier + tier_entered_at writes are non-atomic; concurrent reader can bypass hysteresis","description":"# Bug

backpressure.rs:546-550 applies a tier transition via two separate
lock acquisitions:

    *self.current_tier.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = proposed;
    *self
        .tier_entered_at
        .write()
        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Instant::now();

Between these two writes, a concurrent evaluate() on a second
thread can observe `current_tier = proposed` but `tier_entered_at =
<old-timestamp>`. That thread's hysteresis check at line 540:

    let entered = *self.tier_entered_at.read()...;
    let elapsed_ms = u64::try_from(entered.elapsed().as_millis())...;
    if elapsed_ms < self.config.hysteresis_ms {
        return None;
    }

uses the STALE `entered` value. Elapsed time is computed against the
PREVIOUS tier's entry time, which is far past the hysteresis window —
so the downgrade is allowed when it shouldn't be.

# Scenario

1. T=0: tier=Green, entered_at=T0.
2. T=100s: T1 evaluate() proposes Red (upgrade). Writes
   current_tier=Red. Scheduler pauses T1 before `tier_entered_at`
   write.
3. T=100.001s: T2 evaluate() reads current_tier=Red, proposes Green
   (downgrade). Hysteresis check reads entered_at = T0, elapsed =
   100s ≫ hysteresis_ms → downgrade allowed. Writes
   current_tier=Green.
4. T=100.002s: T1 resumes, writes tier_entered_at=100s.

Final state: current_tier=Green, tier_entered_at=100s. Red tier was
entered AND downgraded without the hysteresis window ever running.

# When does this matter

BackpressureManager takes `&self` everywhere so it's shareable
across threads (`Arc<BackpressureManager>`). Today there's only one
active evaluator task in the runtime (runtime.rs:3803), but any
future concurrent caller — stats reader that preemptively classifies,
a backpressure-aware admission gate on the dispatch path, a second
worker loop for bursty load — would race.

Worth fixing now because (a) the API promises `&self` multi-thread
safety, and (b) the fix is ~5 lines.

# Fix

Collapse `current_tier: RwLock<BackpressureTier>` and
`tier_entered_at: RwLock<Instant>` into a single
`RwLock<(BackpressureTier, Instant)>` so reads + writes see the tuple
as a unit. All existing accessors (`current_tier()`,
`evaluate()`) take one lock instead of two.

# Test

`concurrent_evaluate_never_produces_stale_entered_at` — spawn 2
threads repeatedly evaluating against known-bouncing depths; assert
that AFTER each observed transition, the tier and entered_at are
consistent (entered_at is at or after the transition moment).

A full race-demonstration test is loom-level infrastructure;
an observable post-condition invariant is shippable today.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:29.956478Z","created_by":"pane3-bp-atomic","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","concurrency","hysteresis"]}
 {"id":"ft-8ihuz","title":"session_restore::load_scrollback_data loads ALL segments up to max_seq before truncating, memory-blows on large scrollbacks","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:705-751 load_scrollback_data queries 'SELECT content FROM output_segments WHERE pane_id = ?1 AND seq <= ?2 ORDER BY seq ASC' with NO LIMIT. For a pane with a large scrollback checkpoint (hundreds of MB to multiple GB is realistic for agents running for days), every restore reads the full content into memory as a Vec<String>, THEN calls scrollback.truncate(max_lines) at line 746 which drops everything except the last N. Default restore_max_lines = 5000 (session_restore.rs:142), so for 1M segments totaling 1 GB, we allocate 1 GB and keep ~5 KB. The allocation spike can OOM frankenterm on startup of a large session. Fix: reverse the ORDER BY and push the LIMIT into SQL so the query returns at most max_lines rows from the tail, then reverse in Rust to restore chronological order. This bounds memory at max_lines × avg_segment_size (~ tens of MB worst case). Same truncation semantics (keep most-recent lines) preserved.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:04.442611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:49.985139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:49.984775Z","close_reason":"Landed in 459fadc4 (fix(session_restore): bound load_scrollback_data by max_lines via SQL LIMIT). Flipped ORDER BY to DESC, added LIMIT ?3 with max_lines, reversed in Rust to restore chronological order. Regression test load_scrollback_data_respects_max_lines_limit_ft_8ihuz pins 5-of-50 clamp + chronological order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8jbpj","title":"wezterm.rs pre-existing compile breakage blocks ft-e34d9.10.5.2","description":"While validating ft-e34d9.10.5.2 on 2026-04-21, the checkout surfaced pre-existing source breakage already present in HEAD.

Primary blocker:

Related hygiene blocker surfaced in the same lane:

Evidence:
- dirty diff against HEAD shows removing the stray send_paste line in wezterm.rs and the stray ); in mux_client.rs restores parsable source shape locally.
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p7-orch-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cancellation_during_ --features asupersync-runtime,vendored -- --nocapture surfaced pre-existing frankenterm-core compile failures during the same validation lane.

This bug is blocking clean verification progress on ft-e34d9.10.5.2 support work and should be fixed before further vendored mux migration validation claims are made.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:15.535866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556225Z","close_reason":"Fixed by 9e1bd63b (ft-xbnl0.2.5 bulk cfg strip) — stray pool.send_paste and mux_client.rs stray ); both removed with the non-cx fallback branches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-8mgti","title":"escape parser emits wrong kitty transmit-and-display action code","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:05:01.954836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:08.245193Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:08.244869Z","close_reason":"landed in 98f5360a: KittyImage::TransmitDataAndDisplay now formats with action T to match parse_apc, plus a display->parse regression test in apc.rs; verified locally with scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib kitty_transmit_and_display_display_roundtrips_via_parse_apc -- --nocapture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8qd0l","title":"sync_fts_for_pane skips seq=0 segment on fresh pane (off-by-one in WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq)","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15884 sync_fts_for_pane`:

```rust
let progress = get_fts_pane_progress_sync(conn, pane_id)?;
let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);
```

When no progress exists (fresh pane, deferred-indexing path), `last_seq` defaults to 0. Then `get_unindexed_segments_sync` queries with `WHERE seq > ?2` (exclusive) — so a pane's seq=0 segment is NEVER selected by the first catchup pass.

`append_segment_sync` (storage.rs:12317) computes next_seq via `COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0)` so every pane's FIRST segment is seq=0. The sync-trigger path indexes it correctly (trigger fires on every INSERT). The deferred-catchup path does not.

# Discovery

Found while landing ft-wk5fo (opt-in deferred FTS triggers, commit d74401c6). The initial ft-wk5fo regression tests used seq 0..500 and failed with `99 expected 100` because the first round missed seq=0. Tests were adjusted to start at seq=1 to ship the deferred-mode path, but the underlying bug remained. This bead tracks fixing the bug proper.

# Fix options

(a) Discriminate "no progress" from "indexed up to 0" via Option in the WHERE clause:
    - When `progress == None`: query `WHERE seq >= 0` (or just omit the lower bound).
    - When `progress == Some(p)`: query `WHERE seq > p.last_indexed_seq`.
    Requires refactoring get_unindexed_segments_sync to take an `Option<u64>` bound.

(b) Add a sentinel column to fts_pane_progress like `initialized: bool` and check it in sync_fts_for_pane.

(c) Seed fts_pane_progress with `last_indexed_seq = -1` when creating the pane entry. Requires i64 column or underflow trick.

(a) is cleanest.

# Regression test

Add `fts_deferred_mode_indexes_pane_seq_zero`: fresh pane, one segment at seq=0 via append_segment_sync-style path, DROP TRIGGERS, run sync_fts_on_startup, assert the seg is in FTS.

# Severity

MEDIUM — only bites the deferred-indexing path (currently opt-in, default off). Trigger path is unaffected. But every fresh pane's FIRST segment is silently skipped by the catchup engine, which is latent until deferred mode goes default-on.

# Related

- ft-wk5fo (parent): introduced the deferred path that exposes this bug. Closed with seq-starts-at-1 workaround in the regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:21.050180Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.883029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.882777Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.1","title":"CI pipeline: fmt/clippy/test/bench (feature matrix for mcp/web/tui/sync/browser)","description":"# Task: CI pipeline

## Goal
Ensure core quality checks run automatically on changes and failures are diagnosable from logs/artifacts.

## Core checks (blocking)
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`

## Feature matrix smoke (blocking or warn-only; decide explicitly)
Build/test the crate under common feature combinations so optional integrations don’t silently rot.

Baseline matrix (PLAN Appendix A/F driven):
- default features
- `--features mcp`
- `--features web`
- `--features tui`
- `--features sync`
- `--features browser`

Vendored lane (PLAN Appendix H):
- When `--features vendored` exists, CI must at least:
  - `cargo check --all-targets --features vendored`
  - and run a minimal smoke/unit subset that exercises vendored wiring

This is intentionally separated from the ROI decision (`wa-nu4.4.1.6`): once vendored is enabled, CI must keep it from rotting.

## Docs correctness lane
- Add a docs-smoke step/job that keeps README quickstart commands executable (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

## Related CI jobs (separate tasks)
- E2E job for real WezTerm scenarios: `wa-nu4.3.9.6`
- Setup idempotency job (safe dotfile patching in temp home): `wa-nu4.3.9.8`
- Vendored maintenance lane: `wa-nu4.4.1.7`

## Artifacts and logging
- On failure, upload:
  - test logs (stdout/stderr)
  - E2E artifacts (runner logs + captured outputs)
  - benchmark outputs when applicable

## Testing
- CI “tests the tests”:
  - Add a minimal job that prints the exact rustc/cargo versions and the exact feature-matrix commands being executed.
  - Ensure CI captures and uploads logs/artifacts for failing jobs so failures are actionable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI failures are actionable (clear logs, no flaky tests).
- Optional features (especially vendored) do not silently break over time.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.10","title":"Beads hygiene: eliminate bd lint template warnings (Acceptance Criteria headings, missing sections)","description":"# Task: Beads hygiene (template lint to zero)

## Goal
Make the Beads plan itself reliable and self-documenting by ensuring bd lint produces zero template warnings.

This improves:
- onboarding for future contributors
- clarity during implementation
- confidence that tasks have explicit acceptance criteria

## Scope
- For all open tasks/features/bugs:
  - ensure the description includes the required section heading(s):
    - tasks/features: ## Acceptance Criteria
    - bugs: ## Steps to Reproduce, ## Acceptance Criteria
  - ensure the acceptance criteria are concrete and testable

## Change discipline
- Do not delete or truncate existing content.
- Prefer minimal edits:
  - rename "## Acceptance" -> "## Acceptance Criteria"
  - add a missing section at the end if absent

## Acceptance Criteria
- bd lint --status open produces no warnings.
- New issues created in this project follow the template by default.


## Testing
- Run `bd lint` locally after any template edits.
- Add a simple CI check (if desired) that runs `bd lint --status open` and fails on warnings.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.11","title":"CI: shellcheck install.sh + scripts/install-hooks.sh (ship-gate for installer shell)","description":"## Gap

Ship-readiness epic wa-nu4.3.9 covers fmt/clippy/tests/release packaging, but the **public installer shell** (`install.sh` at repo root, the `curl | bash` one-liner target) and `scripts/install-hooks.sh` have zero CI coverage. A subtle bash bug there would break every fresh user install without CI catching it.

## Deliverable

Add a lightweight `installer-shell` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that runs on Ubuntu and:

- `bash -n` syntax-checks `install.sh` and `scripts/install-hooks.sh`
- `shellcheck` (error+warning severity) against both scripts
- Fails the build on any finding

## Why not scripts/*.sh broadly

`scripts/*.sh` is a large internal surface with known style noise; a strict shellcheck pass there is a separate triage task. This bead is scoped to the two scripts that run on end-user machines outside our dev env, where a break = broken install day one.

## Acceptance

- New job visible in Actions tab
- Runs in < 60s (no Rust build)
- Both scripts parse clean under `bash -n`; both pass shellcheck at severity `warning`
- Part of the required ship-readiness gate (no `continue-on-error`)
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:48:23.255444Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:14.620198Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:14.620012Z","close_reason":"CI installer-shell job added (9527b8d5): bash -n + shellcheck --severity=warning on install.sh + scripts/install-hooks.sh. Both clean locally.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","install","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.11","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:48:23.255444Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.12","title":"README: add discoverable Quickstart section (install → verify → first robot call)","description":"## Gap

README.md has a center-aligned `Quick Install` snippet at line ~19 and a deep `## Installation` section at line **236**, but **no cohesive `## Quickstart` walkthrough** that takes a new operator from zero to first useful output.

The closed bead wa-nu4.3.9.2 landed the CLI/robot/MCP reference and wa-nu4.3.9.9 landed docs smoke, but neither established a top-of-README guided path that reads install → verify → first robot call → pointer to next steps.

## Deliverable

Add a `## Quickstart` H2 section in README.md placed **directly after TL;DR / Platform Model** and **before Supported Surface Matrix**, containing:

1. One-command install (`cargo install --git ... --bin ft frankenterm`) + external dep callout (WezTerm CLI must be reachable).
2. Verify step: `ft --version` + `ft doctor --json | jq .` with expected-shape note.
3. First useful command: `ft robot state` emits panes as JSON (swarm-observable in one call).
4. `--format toon` token-efficiency note for AI-to-AI use.
5. `## Next steps` cross-links into the deeper sections already in the README (Quick Example, Installation, Robot Mode API).

## Why

Ship-readiness definition-of-done in wa-nu4.3.9 says: "README + quickstart lets another engineer install and run wa." A walkthrough buried 236 lines deep doesn't satisfy that for a first-touch operator.

## Acceptance

- New `## Quickstart` H2 present and discoverable in the first ~120 lines.
- No duplication: the new section **references** deeper sections, doesn't copy them.
- No changes outside README.md.
- Markdown renders cleanly; code fences use `bash` / `console` / `json` as appropriate.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:33:52.308093091Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:51:21.358065451Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:51:21.357974873Z","close_reason":"Added docs/cli-reference.md with human/robot/MCP command matrix, feature-gated/planned notes, and MCP tool/resource list","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","title":"Version/build metadata: `wa --version` shows semver + build info + enabled features","description":"# Task: Version/build metadata (`wa --version`)

## Goal
Make it easy to know exactly what build of wa is running when debugging bugs, comparing machines, or filing issues.

This becomes non-optional once we span multiple machines and (optionally) a vendored WezTerm build.

## Output contract
- `wa --version` prints a **single, stable** line by default (human-friendly, scriptable).
- `wa --version --verbose` prints a multi-line block with detailed metadata.
- Output must not require a TTY.

## Fields to include (when available)
- `wa` semver
- build timestamp (UTC)
- git commit hash (if built from a git checkout)
- rustc version
- target triple
- enabled cargo features (mcp/web/tui/sync/browser/vendored/etc.)

Vendored mode (PLAN Appendix H):
- when `--features vendored` is enabled, include:
  - vendored WezTerm commit hash (the pinned commit)
  - any compatibility/version string we can reliably extract

## Why this matters
When automation spans multiple machines, “what version is running?” is the first diagnostic question. Vendored mode adds an additional critical dimension (which WezTerm commit?).

## Testing
- Unit tests for `--version` output:
  - non-empty
  - contains semver
  - stable (no ANSI, no random ordering)

- Verbose output tests:
  - includes the detailed fields and feature list
  - when built with vendored feature (in CI matrix), includes vendored commit hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa --version` output is stable and does not require a TTY.
- `wa --version --verbose` provides enough metadata to uniquely identify the build.
- Vendored builds surface vendored commit metadata deterministically.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.4","title":"Release packaging: reproducible builds + single-binary install path","description":"# Task: Release packaging

## Goal
Provide a simple, reliable way for users to install and update wa:
- build a single lean binary
- ship artifacts with enough metadata to debug
- keep installation friction low

## Deliverables
- Release build produces a single binary (`cargo build --release`).
- Provide at least one installation path:
  - downloadable release artifact (preferred)
  - optional `cargo install --path .` for devs

- Document:
  - minimum supported OSes
  - required external deps (e.g., WezTerm installed)
  - feature flags and what they enable

## Reproducibility & safety
- Aim for reproducible builds (same inputs → same hash) where feasible.
- Publish checksums for release artifacts.
- Never require users to run unsafe install commands.

## Testing
- Smoke tests for the released binary:
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment)

- Feature-matrix build verification in CI (tracked in CI bead):
  - default features
  - `--features mcp`
  - `--features web`
  - `--features tui`
  - `--features sync`
  - `--features browser`
  - when vendored exists: `--features vendored`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can install and run `wa --help` without cloning the repo (via a release artifact).
- Release artifacts include build metadata (`wa --version --verbose` is meaningful).

@@ -65,6 +65,54 @@ Current implementation reality:
 
 ---
 
+## Quickstart
+
+Zero to first useful output in four commands.
+
+### 1. Install
+
+```bash
+cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git --bin ft frankenterm
+```
+
+External requirement: `ft` talks to a live mux through the current WezTerm interop boundary, so `wezterm` must be in `PATH` and `wezterm cli list --format json` must succeed before pane-read/write operations will work. See [Installation](#installation) for from-source and feature-gated builds.
+
+### 2. Verify
+
+```bash
+ft --version                 # semver + git commit + rustc + target + features
+ft doctor --json | jq .      # environment checks as structured JSON
+```
+
+`ft doctor` exits non-zero on missing prerequisites; treat that as the authoritative signal that the environment isn't ready yet.
+
+### 3. First robot call
+
+```bash
+ft robot state               # list every pane the current mux can see, as JSON
+ft robot --format toon state # same, TOON-compact — use this when piping to an AI
+```
+
+This is the single call an AI agent makes to get a full snapshot of the swarm's panes without starting the watch daemon.
+
+### 4. Watch, then react
+
+```bash
+ft watch --foreground        # run the capture/pattern/workflow daemon
+# in another shell:
+ft robot events --limit 10   # recent pattern-triggered events
+ft robot search "error"      # full-text search across captured output
+```
+
+### Next steps
+
+- [Quick Example](#quick-example) — fuller command tour (send, wait-for, mission, tx)
+- [Installation](#installation) — from-source build and optional features (`mcp`, `web`, `distributed`, `browser`)
+- [Robot Mode (JSON API)](#robot-mode-json-api) if you jumped here — the contract AI agents use to drive other AI agents
+- [Supported Surface Matrix](#supported-surface-matrix) — honest status of every shipped surface
+
